title: Mixer Initialization, Channels, Latency, Volume, Mute, and Cue Alternatives · Language Clinic: Volume Controls
summary: Audio initializes late with wrong parameters, rapid shots cut off critical warnings, one slider controls everything, mute is unavailable, missing audio crashes launch, and gameplay meaning is sound-only. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the volume controls increment in bounded accessible audio and cue system. Learners progress through predi…
objectives: Provide master and separate effects and music control, mute, remembered settings, and safe defaults.
Reserve or prioritize channels for critical cues and define overlap, stealing, cooldown, and device-loss behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-audio-mixer-cues-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-audio-mixer-cues-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_audio_mixer_cues_3(critical_events: int = 5, visual_alternatives: int = 5, channels_reserved: int = 3, clipped_sounds: int = 0, mute_available: bool = True, separate_volume: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if critical_events < 0 or channels_reserved < 1:
        return False, critical_events, "audio-domain"
    if visual_alternatives != critical_events:
        return False, visual_alternatives, "cue-alternative"
    if clipped_sounds > 0:
        return False, clipped_sounds, "channel-starvation"
    if not mute_available or not separate_volume:
        return False, channels_reserved, "audio-control"
    assert visual_alternatives == critical_events
    return True, channels_reserved, "accessible-audio"

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-volume-controls-1alrr8" that uses case 4779 to own mixer initialization, channel budgets, latency, volume and mute controls, and non-audio alternatives for every critical cue. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-volume-controls-1alrr8
# Competency: asteroids-volume-controls.
# Case 4779: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def evidence_asteroids_volume_controls_1alrr8(critical_events: int = 5, visual_alternatives: int = 5, channels_reserved: int = 3, clipped_sounds: int = 0, mute_available: bool = True, separate_volume: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1alrr8 = "1alrr8-8rrla1-4779"
    assert evidence_variant_1alrr8
    evidence_axes_1alrr8 = (
        "player1alrr8",
        "seed4779",
        "input8rrla1",
        "frame1alrr84779",
        "state47791alrr8",
        "playtest8rrla14779",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1alrr8)) == 6
    if critical_events < 0 or channels_reserved < 1:
        return False, critical_events, "audio-domain"
    if visual_alternatives != critical_events:
        return False, visual_alternatives, "cue-alternative"
    if clipped_sounds > 0:
        return False, clipped_sounds, "channel-starvation"
    if not mute_available or not separate_volume:
        return False, channels_reserved, "audio-control"
    assert visual_alternatives == critical_events
    return True, channels_reserved, "accessible-audio"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Volume Controls model · Theory Volume Controls
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Volume Controls: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 4288 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to own mixer initialization, channel budgets, latency, volume and mute controls, and non-audio alternatives for every critical cue; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Provide master and separate effects and music control, mute, remembered settings, and safe defaults.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Volume Controls model · Theory Volume Controls.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Volume Controls model · Theory Volume Controls, keep Volume Controls connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Provide master and separate effects and music control, mute, remembered settings, and safe defaults.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Volume Controls model · Theory Volume Controls, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Volume Controls precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Volume Controls model · Theory Volume Controls, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 4288 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to own mixer initialization, channel budgets, latency, volume and mute controls, and non-audio alternatives for every critical cue; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Provide master and separate effects and music control, mute, remembered settings, and safe defaults.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Volume Controls model · Theory Volume Controls, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Operating-system volume is enough game audio control.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: asteroids-volume-controls-1alrr8.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: asteroids-volume-controls-1alrr8(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_asteroids_volume_controls_1alrr8\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?visual_alternatives != critical_events)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?clipped_sounds > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not mute_available)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not separate_volume)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
